messageFlowGroups.label: Conversation
messageFlowGroups.description: Complete conversation lifecycle: invite -> accept/decline -> walk -> chat -> end. NPCs auto-accept invites. Messages trigger NPC replies via Claude.

messageFlowGroups.label: Movement
messageFlowGroups.description: Two movement systems: WASD keys (input_start/stop -> velocity + collision) and click-to-move (move -> A* pathfinding). Both produce player_update broadcasts.

messageFlowGroups.label: Lifecycle
messageFlowGroups.description: Player connection and disconnection. Join spawns a player at a map spawn point. Disconnect cleans up conversations and removes the player.

stateMachines.label: Conversation Lifecycle
stateMachines.description: Governs the lifecycle of a conversation between two players. NPCs auto-accept invites (no client UI). Walking state navigates both players toward a rendezvous point. Active state allows message exchange until timeout, max messages, or manual end.

stateMachines.label: Player State
stateMachines.description: Governs what a player is currently doing. Movement and conversations are mutually exclusive: a conversing player cannot move, and starting movement cancels any pending path. The 'doing_activity' state is for future activity interactions.

transitions.condition: Future: player interacts with map activity
Future: player finishes or leaves activity

dataStructures.name: Memory
dataStructures.summary: Application-level memory (converted from {@link MemoryRow}). Represents a single unit of NPC knowledge — an observation, a conversation summary, or a higher-level reflection synthesized from other memories.

fields.name: playerId
fields.description: The NPC who owns this memory.

fields.name: type
fields.description: observation: something the NPC saw; conversation: a chat summary; reflection: a synthesized insight.

fields.name: importance
fields.description: 1–10 scale of how significant this memory is (drives reflection threshold and retrieval ranking).

fields.name: embedding
fields.description: pgvector embedding for semantic similarity search (1536-dim by default).

fields.name: relatedIds
fields.description: IDs of memories this one was derived from (used by reflections to cite source memories).

fields.name: tick
fields.description: Game tick when this memory was created. Used for recency scoring (exponential decay).

fields.name: lastAccessedTick
fields.description: Last tick this memory was retrieved; throttled updates avoid write amplification.

dataStructures.name: ScoredMemory
dataStructures.summary: Memory with composite retrieval score for ranking. Produced by MemoryManager.retrieveMemories() after re-ranking vector search candidates.
dataStructures.purpose: Memory with composite retrieval score for ranking.

fields.name: score
fields.description: Composite score: recencyScore + importanceScore + relevanceScore. Higher is better.

fields.name: recencyScore
fields.description: Exponential decay based on ticks since creation: 0.99^ticksAgo.

fields.name: relevanceScore
fields.description: Cosine similarity between the query embedding and this memory's embedding.

dataStructures.name: Player
dataStructures.summary: The full mutable state of a player (human or NPC) within the simulation. Movement is driven by two mutually-exclusive systems: 1. **Path following** — A* sets `path`/`pathIndex`/`targetX`/`targetY`. The player moves along waypoints at `pathSpeed` tiles per tick. 2. **Held input** — WASD keys set `inputX`/`inputY`, producing velocity (`vx`/`vy`) at `inputSpeed` tiles per second, resolved with collision.
dataStructures.purpose: The full mutable state of a player (human or NPC) within the simulation.

fields.name: isWaitingForResponse
fields.description: True while the NPC model is generating a reply (shows "..." bubble).

fields.name: targetX
fields.description: A* destination tile X (set by click-to-move or conversation rendezvous).

fields.name: path
fields.description: Waypoints computed by A*; consumed one-by-one during path movement.

fields.name: pathSpeed
fields.description: Tiles per tick when following an A* path.

fields.name: vx
fields.description: Current X velocity (tiles/sec) — set by input movement.

fields.name: vy
fields.description: Current Y velocity (tiles/sec) — set by input movement.

fields.name: inputSpeed
fields.description: Tiles per second when moving via keyboard input.

fields.name: hp
fields.description: Current hit points. Undefined means full health (backwards-compatible).

fields.name: maxHp
fields.description: Maximum hit points. Undefined defaults to PLAYER_DEFAULT_HP (100).

system.label: AI Town
system.description: Runtime applications and data stores that make up the AI Town software system.

containers.name: Browser Client
containers.description: Browser application that renders the town, captures player input, predicts local movement, and keeps the player UI synchronized with server state.
responsibilities: Renders the town, actors, and browser UI.
Captures player input and performs local movement prediction.
Sends commands to the server and applies server updates.

componentTargets.reason: Open the Browser Client boundary in the Components view.

containers.summary: Thin browser shell around the authoritative server. Owns rendering, browser-side input, and prediction.

openNext.label: Browser Client boundary
openNext.reason: Shows the browser-side component cards and their surrounding runtime context.

openNext.label: App Shell
openNext.reason: Best next hop for browser bootstrap, network wiring, reconciliation, and player input handling.

containers.name: Game Server
containers.description: Authoritative application that owns runtime state, transport surfaces, debug routes, NPC orchestration, and access to persistent and file-backed data.
responsibilities: Owns the authoritative simulation, movement rules, and conversations.
Exposes WebSocket and HTTP/debug interfaces to the browser.
Coordinates NPC behavior plus reads and writes backing data stores.

componentTargets.reason: Open the Game Server boundary in the Components view.

containers.summary: Single-process authoritative server. Owns gameplay state, WebSocket/HTTP surfaces, and NPC orchestration.

openNext.label: Game Server boundary
openNext.reason: Shows the server-side component cards and how they interact.

openNext.label: Simulation Core
openNext.reason: Best next hop for authoritative state and simulation behavior.

openNext.label: WebSocket Gateway
openNext.reason: Best next hop for client/server transport handling.

containers.name: PostgreSQL + pgvector
containers.description: Relational datastore for memories, conversations, messages, players, and LLM generation metadata, including vector similarity search over embeddings.
responsibilities: Stores memories, conversations, messages, players, and generation records.
Supports similarity lookup through pgvector indexes.
Acts as an optional durable backing store at runtime.

componentTargets.reason: Persistence component owns the database-facing code paths and schema facts.
NPC stack reads and writes memories and generations through persistence.

containers.summary: Optional durable memory and conversation store with pgvector-backed similarity search.

openNext.label: Persistence Adapters
openNext.reason: Best next hop for repository/store implementations and schema usage.

openNext.label: NPC Orchestration
openNext.reason: Shows where memory retrieval and generation persistence are triggered.

openNext.label: schema.sql
openNext.reason: See the concrete tables and pgvector index backing the datastore.

containers.name: World Data Files
containers.description: Repo-owned files containing map geometry, activities, spawn points, and NPC seed data used during startup and map serving.
responsibilities: Stores map geometry, activities, and spawn points.
Stores default NPC seed definitions owned by the repo.
Provides file-backed startup data rather than a separate service.

componentTargets.reason: ENGINE loads world state from the map file at startup.
Browser bootstrap fetches map content for rendering and prediction.
Debug routes expose and serve map-related state.

containers.summary: Static world and NPC seed data stored in repo-owned files.

evidence.detail: main.ts initializes renderer, fetches map/debug data, and connects the WebSocket client.
GameClient opens a browser WebSocket connection to the game server.
index.ts wires the Express app, GameLoop, NPC stack, debug router, and WebSocket server.
GameWebSocketServer translates browser messages into engine actions and sends server messages back out.
Debug router exposes HTTP read/control surfaces for local inspection and mutation.
schema.sql defines the persistent tables and indexes for the datastore.
Schema defines tables: world, activities, players, conversations, messages, memories, llm_generations, game_log.
Schema flags: vector(1536), IVFFlat memory index.
map.json stores world geometry, activities, and spawn points.
Shared characters.ts stores default NPC seed data in the repo root.
Server-local characters.ts is the seed file imported by the runtime at startup.
